title: 222 Subtitle Transfer
wikitext:
[]: 2.22 Subtitle Transfer?             Quote Post by Zekkykins » Mon Sep 06, 2010 12:26 am Honestly, I didn't know exactly where to put this, but the Rebuild section seemed like the most logical. Well, I got my hands on the 2.22 Blu-Ray release with English subtitles, and.. Well, to be honest, I think the subtitles are absolute rubbish . I honestly preferred the fansubs. So, I was wondering.. Would it be possible to - in any way, shape, or form - actually swap out the subs on the 2.22 disc with the fansubs? If this is absolutely stupid question, I wouldn't be surprised at all, and feel free to let this topic die a slow, painful death. But, if this actually is possible, please lemme know with details on how to do this?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Sep 06, 2010 12:36 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by Zekkykins » Mon Sep 06, 2010 12:43 am Suppose I'll just have to wait to get a computer back into my room and just hook up the computer to the TV. Ahh, patience. 'Tis not a virtue I have. Thanks for gettin' back to me so quickly.

[]:             Quote Post by squall.leonhart » Mon Sep 13, 2010 1:11 am This is entirely possible with the use of a Blu-ray burner and a blank 50GiB BD-R. Decrypt the entire disc structure to your hard-drive using AnyDVD-HD. The file should be about 40+GiB. Open MultiAVCHD and import the disc folder into reauthor mode. Because Khara used BD-J, it will be impossible to recreate the full disc structure so you can only really focus on the movie. MultiAVCHD will scan each title and place them in the project window. Open each title and delete the ones you don't need (such as annoying advertisements, trailers, or special features). You should be left with the main movie title. Click the tab for external subs and import an SRT subtitle file (ASS will be accepted, but it screws with override tags so it's not recommended). Press Accept when finished tinkering with the title properties. Set any project properties you may need, such as menus (or just check play first title), maybe change the subtitle style, set your output folder, and press start. The result will be a Blu-ray structured folder ready for imaging into UDF 2.50 and then burning, both with IMGBurn. tSmuxer can do the same thing with a decrypted file, but the resulting folder may not play in all set-top players because of text subtitle support. MultiAVCHD will create image sub files (SUP) that are readable in any player. I would see if your player supports USB or SD card playback of video files with subtitles. Most newer players do (like the Samsung BD-C5500C). Then you can buy a 64GiB flash drive and remux the raw files onto that. It would be cheaper than the burner solution and easier by far.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Mon Sep 13, 2010 1:36 am yep, that sounds about right... there is something else i could sudgest but let me get back to you on that. If i'm wrong i don't want to waste your time
